check: coarse + medium REACH M0.84 and are clean discrete solutions
value: coarse: reached M0.84; M_max 1.51; 0 over cap; conv 1; medium: reached M0.84; M_max 2.00; 0 over cap; conv 1
criterion: ramp reaches M0.84; 0 over cap / 0 lim / 0 flr / converged
note: the round cap is transonically fine at these two levels (M_max 1.51 / 2.00; well under the cap)

check: ★★ NEGATIVE RESULT: the FINE mesh cannot REACH M0.84 at all
value: the Mach ramp BREAKS at M=0.7500 (last converged M=0.7375; 14 levels attempted) — it never gets to M0.80; let alone M0.84
criterion: target M0.84 NOT reached on the fine round mesh
note: ★ THIS IS THE FINDING; and it is sharper than 'the fine solve did not converge'. There is NO M0.84 fine state on the round mesh at all: the continuation dm-halves at M=0.75 until dm < dm_min and gives up (1 cell in the density floor; |R| stalling ~8e-6). Reading any census at M_INF off that state applies the WRONG freestream Mach — an earlier version of this demo did exactly that and manufactured a spurious 'M_max 3.97 / 5 over cap at M0.84'. The guard in solve() now refuses to census a state whose ramp did not reach the target

check: ★ SITE = the sharp tip TE; the round cap does not CREATE it; it HEATS it
value: the 20 fastest cells on the failed fine field: 20/20 on the SHARP TIP TE (z/b 0.97-0.99; chord-frac ~0.998); 0/20 on the rounded cap (z/b > 1)  |  and the round M_max is higher at EVERY completed level: 1.51 / 2.00 (round) vs 1.39 / 1.73 (flat)
criterion: the singular site is the TE; shared by both families; what the round cap changes is the SPEED there
note: ★ THE RECONCILIATION. The flat fine DOES complete the ramp (M_max 2.818; 0 over cap; cl_KJ 0.2866) on the SAME refinement level; so at first the shared tip TE looks exonerated. It is not: `run_g133_roundtip_transonic_locate.py` puts every one of the 20 fastest cells ON the tip TE and NONE on the new cap surface. The rounded tip lets flow WRAP AROUND the tip and accelerate — which raises the velocity at that same; pre-existing sharp TE at every level — and at fine that pushes it past the limiter; where the flat cap's cooler tip flow stays under. So the cap did not add a singularity; it amplified the one that was already there. (Positions are valid on a failed-level field because argsort(Mach) = argsort(|q|) for any m_inf; the Mach VALUES on that state are not physical.)

check: P9 BAND VERDICT: NOT EARNED — there is no third point
value: M0.84 lift exists only at coarse (0.2769) and medium (0.2763) (flat-norm; -0.21%); the fine mesh has no M0.84 state at all
criterion: two points are not a Richardson; no extrapolation; no band read
note: P9/G9.3 discipline. Coarse and medium agree to 0.2%; which is suggestive but is NOT a three-point convergence study. ⇒ the P9 band verdict is not earned on the round cap. On the FLAT cap it is not earned either: those solves DO converge but the sequence 0.2593 → 0.2652 → 0.2866 RISES (non-asymptotic; and on the clamped ladder); so 0.2866 is a single reported point; not a Richardson. ⇒ the 'the 0.019 gap is resolution ⇒ P11 refuted' conclusion has no clean asymptotic basis on EITHER geometry

check: HONESTY: s_ref differs between the families
value: coarse: round 0.7602 vs flat 0.7532; medium: round 0.7602 vs flat 0.7532; fine: round 0.7602 vs flat 0.7532
criterion: recorded -- the band is read on the flat-renormalised cl
note: the rounded cap adds ~0.9% planform area; cl_kj is round-norm; cl_kj_flatnorm renormalises onto the flat s_ref for the 0.288 comparison
